title: Preventing Middleware-Induced Route Conflicts in Express

statement.inputFormat: The input format is not applicable for this task.
statement.outputFormat: The output format is not applicable for this task.
statement.notes: The API should respond to GET requests for /api, /api/user, /api/user/:id, and /api/product. The middleware ordering should be corrected to prevent conflicts.

examples.explanation: The API responds to a GET request for /api.
The API responds to a GET request for /api/user.
The API responds to a GET request for /api/user/:id.
The API responds to a GET request for /api/product.

hints.text: The middleware ordering should be corrected to prevent conflicts between routes.

editorial.title: Preventing Middleware-Induced Route Conflicts in Express
editorial.intuition: The middleware ordering should be corrected to prevent conflicts between routes. The correct order should ensure that the more specific routes are handled before the less specific routes.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to try all possible middleware orderings and see which one works.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the Express Router to handle the routes. This will ensure that the more specific routes are handled before the less specific routes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the API responds to all the expected requests without any middleware-induced route conflicts.
